# Exit codes

`ost` maps each failure to a stable [`Category`] and a deterministic process exit code, so CI can branch on the *kind* of failure. Under `--json` the category also appears as `error.category`.

| Exit code | Category | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | (success) | The command succeeded. |
| 2 | `usage` | Bad arguments or usage. |
| 3 | `configuration` | Invalid manifest, lock, or configuration. |
| 4 | `precondition` | A missing prerequisite: runtime, tool, or directory. |
| 5 | `validation` | A validation mismatch (lock check, validate, plugin test). |
| 6 | `external_tool` | An external tool failed (CMake, Ninja, compiler, OpenUSD). |
| 7 | `io` | Filesystem or permission error. |
| 70 | `internal` | An unexpected internal error. |
